whatItIs: The Internet Archive's Wayback Machine and similar services keep snapshots of old pages — profiles, bylines, directory listings under your former name — long after the live page is gone or changed.
actions: Once the live page is removed or updated, request exclusion of the archived snapshots: email info@archive.org from an address tied to the page or its domain, listing the exact archived URLs and why they should come down.
For pages you control, a robots.txt rule or a removal request can keep them from being re-archived.
For snapshots that can't be excluded, keep a list and re-check periodically — archives sometimes honor requests later.
disclaimer: Informational only. Web archives are independent and may decline; there is no guaranteed removal.
